title: Apply Consistent Gridline Styling to All DataTables Globally
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want all data tables to have consistent gridline styling, so that the interface looks polished and uniform across all pages.
description:
DataTables across the application had inconsistent border/gridline styling. Some tables had visible gridlines while others did not, creating a disjointed visual experience.

## CSS Fix
Two global CSS overrides applied to all DataTable instances:
- `table.dataTable.no-footer` — adds a consistent bottom border (`1px solid #ddd`)
- `table.dataTable thead th, table.dataTable thead td` — adds a consistent bottom border to header cells

Both rules use `!important` to override DataTables default styles and ensure uniform gridlines across all table views.
acceptance_criteria: All DataTable instances display consistent gridline borders
Table footer borders are uniform across all pages
Table header cell borders are uniform across all pages
No visual regressions on existing table layouts
ux_changes: Applied global CSS overrides to DataTables for consistent gridline borders. All tables now show uniform 1px solid #ddd borders on header cells and table footers.
implementation_notes: Two CSS rules added globally with !important to override DataTables default styles: border-bottom on .no-footer class and on thead th/td elements. Uses #ddd color for subtle gridlines.
comment_summary: Developer implemented the CSS changes and pushed to staging for review in incognito mode.
